title: Hemodynamic and Clinical Changes Over 90 Days of Dual Antiplatelet Therapy in Ischemic Stroke Patients with Intracranial Atherosclerotic Stenosis: A Prospective Pre–Post Transcranial Doppler Study
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"tckbp","text":"**Background: To report such changes in parameters of TCD (MFV and PI) and clinical outcomes during 90 days in patients with ischemic stroke receiving dual antiplatelet therapy (DAPT) with the aid of ICAS.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"krq1i","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"irvsi","text":"**Methods: We used a prospective observational pre-post study among patients with ischemic stroke and using ICAS that received 90 days of DAPT intervention in a tertiary referral center. At the baseline and day 90, National Institutes of Health Stroke Scale (NIHSS), the Indonesian version of the Montreal Cognitive Assessment (MoCA-Ina), and TCD of the middle cerebral artery were conducted to obtain the MFV and PI. Paired comparisons were performed using the paired t-test or the Wilcoxon signed-rank test, while associations between variables were assessed using Pearson or Spearman correlation analyses.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"tevp9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"oo3yp","text":"**Results: This study Findings Sixty-nine patients were involved (66.7% men; mean age 57 years). MFV also improved (40 ++ 22 to 93 ++ 13 cm/s; p 3c 0.001), and so did NIHSS (median 4 [4-6] to 4 [3-4]; p 3c 0.001) and MoCA-Ina (27 [24-29] to 28 [26-29]; p 3c 0.001), after more than 90 days. Conversely, PI did not exhibit any significant difference (2.37 ++ 1.28 to 2.30 ++ 1.37; p = 0.684).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"am3fn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"xxvnr","text":"**Conclusions: We found significant reductions in MFV, and clinical outcomes in 90 days in ischemic stroke patients with ICAS who received DAPT, but not PI. The implication of this dissociation is an improvement of large-vessel hemodynamics more than distal vascular resistance, and possibly in the elucidation of post-stroke recovery in ICAS.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: The study employed a prospective observational pre-post design and was performed at Dr. Hasan Sadikin General Hospital, a tertiary referral center in Bandung, Indonesia. The measure of this research was the comparative changes in clinical and transcranial Doppler (TCD) parameters between the ischemic stroke patients with intracranial atherosclerotic stenosis (ICAS) receiving dual antiplatelet therapy (DAPT) over a 90-day time span.

We enrolled in turn adult stroke patients who experienced an ischemic stroke and had evidence of intracranial arterial stenosis and were treated with DAPT in routine secondary prevention. Patients were included based on diagnosis of ischemic stroke, documented intracranial arterial stenosis on neurovascular studies, and completion of 90-day follow up. Inclusion criteria were: (1) cardioembolic stroke, (2) extracranial carotid stenosis as the main etiology, (3) the absence of temporal acoustic window because of which a reliable TCD examination is not possible, and (4) severe comorbidity or clinical instability, which makes it impossible to assess the patient further. The patient selection process is illustrated in Figure 1.
Figure 1. Flow Chart of Patient Recruitment.

All the patients were given dual antiplatelet therapy as per institutional protocols and modern guidelines, comprising aspirin and clopidogrel, with an estimated treatment of 90 days. There was the possibility of other standard secondary prevention, which consisted of risk factor control and statin therapy, which were applied at the discretion of the treating physicians.

The evaluations were conducted at baseline (before or on the admission of DAPT) and 90 days. Neurological impairment and global cognitive status were evaluated using the National Institutes of Health Stroke Scale and the Indonesian version of the Montreal Cognitive Assessment. Transcranial Doppler ultrasonography of the middle cerebral artery (MCA) was used to evaluate cerebral hemodynamics. The main TCD parameters of interest included mean flow velocity (MFV), which is a proxy of large-vessel flow and stenosis-induced hemodynamic variation, and pulsatility index (PI), which is (PSV - EDV)/MFV, which is an indirect measure of the distal vascular resistance. All tests were carried out by certified staff on a standardized procedure.

Data with continuous distribution are reported as mean ± standard deviation or median (interquartile range) according to normality. Categorical data are expressed as number and percentages. Pre-post comparisons were analyzed using paired t-test for normally distributed variables and Wilcoxon signed-rank test for non-normally distributed variables. Correlations between clinical and hemodynamic variables were explored using Pearson or Spearman correlation coefficients. Statistical significance was defined as a two-sided p value of less than 0.05 and analyses were conducted using standard statistical software.
Approval was obtained from the institutional ethics board, and the study was conducted in compliance with the Declaration of Helsinki. Informed consent obtained from every participant.
creator: aninditosidhy
